4.anchor: And Esther said, “If it please the king,
4.note: Hebrew If it is good to the king

8.anchor: If I have found favor in the sight of the king, and if it please the king

6.anchor: There were white cotton curtains and violet hangings fastened with cords of fine linen and purple to silver rods

17.anchor: For the queen’s behavior will be made known to all women, causing them to look at their husbands with contempt,
17.note: Hebrew to disdain their husbands in their eyes

2.note: is an alternate spelling of Bigthana Bigthan

5.anchor: Then King Ahasuerus said to Queen Esther, “Who is he, and where is he, who has dared
5.note: Hebrew whose heart has filled him

9.anchor: whose word saved the king, is standing at Haman’s house, fifty cubits

13.anchor: tomorrow also to do according to this day’s edict. And let the ten sons of Haman be hanged on the gallows.”

4.anchor: And let the young woman who pleases the king
4.note: Hebrew who is good in the eyes of the king

23.anchor: When the affair was investigated and found to be so, the men were both hanged on the gallows.

6.anchor: to lay hands on Mordecai alone. So, as they had made known to him the people of Mordecai, Haman sought to destroy

9.anchor: If it please the king, let it be decreed that they be destroyed, and I will pay 10,000 talents

7.anchor: I have given Esther the house of Haman, and they have hanged him on the gallows,

15.anchor: in royal robes of blue and white, with a great golden crown

8.anchor: a copy of the written decree issued in Susa for their destruction,
